title: TMYK
description:
The more you know...
Search the Minecraft Wiki through chat.
body:
_(formerly known as "Wigot")_

Need some quick info? You could open your browser and type "minecraft wiki wither skeleton" and then try to make sure you click on the link to the correct wiki...OR you could just type "/wiki wither skeleton" in game and have it give you a clickable link right where you want to go. Much nicer. Think of the seconds of your life that are being saved. That'll add up over time. Although, the more I write here, the more of those seconds have to be accrued to offset this paragraph. Oh geez, okay uh, bye.

There is one command with two uses:

1. Use "/wiki" followed by your search term(s). (Spaces are fine--no quotes needed. e.g. "/wiki creeper farm")
![Wiki Search Results](https://fg83.net/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/wigot_1.0_screenshot.png)
2. Use "/wiki" with no arguments while looking at any block/entity/fluid within reach.
![Wiki search results (proximity)](https://fg83.net/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/wigot_1.1_screenshot.png)


## 🍷Cubium - The Perfect Pairing
For a seamless browsing experience in-game, try HenriTom's mod [Cubium](https://modrinth.com/mod/cubium) to bring your research with you as you adventure.
